mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding household income and consumption demand, leading to upward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above six percent indicates significant labor market slack that delays Federal Reserve tightening, sustaining low discount rates and liquidity support that generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time or the average return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production validate a synchronized expansion of final demand and manufacturing output, reducing recession probability and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: In months when both RSAFS and INDPRO increase, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time or the information coefficient of the interaction term is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound monetary regime identified by a federal funds rate below 0.25 percent, month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation reflect reflationary demand growth without offsetting tightening, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, months with rising CPIAUCSL fail to produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and operational leverage, which translates into expanding corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million units demonstrate robust residential investment and durable consumer demand, which spills over into construction, durable goods, and credit growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances or the mean return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirm broad-based recovery in labor income and residential investment, amplifying aggregate demand expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: In months when both PAYEMS and HOUST increase, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time or the interaction term's rank correlation with returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month cumulative increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor income and contracting aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over two months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative less than 55% of the time or the average return is not significantly below zero.
